qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Theme ZIP upload permits low-privilege PHP execution from the public templates directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a separate theme installer flaw in `Modules\Theme\Controllers\Theme::upload` and `install_theme_from_tmp()` that places attacker-controlled PHP under `public/templates/<theme>/`. The advisory is a Zip Slip in `Modules\Backup\Controllers\Backup::restore` that extracts backup ZIP entries outside `writable/uploads/` before the 0.31.5.0 fix.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described `Backup::restore` Zip Slip root cause. The only qualifying finding is a different authenticated theme-upload-to-PHP-execution bug.
evidence: The advisory describes `modules/Backup/Controllers/Backup.php` calling `ZipArchive::extractTo()` on uploaded backup ZIP files without validating entry names, with the vulnerable route bound at `POST backend/backup/restore` and exempted from CSRF checks.

The only qualifying session finding instead cites `modules/Theme/Controllers/Theme.php`, `modules/Theme/Helpers/themes_helper.php`, and `public/.htaccess` as the exploit path for moving attacker-controlled files into `public/templates/<theme>/` and executing them directly.

The finding's reproduction report states the PoC was rerun successfully on commit `418f0bf2b04ee9b046ea296c55d31466ecd0c919` (tag `0.31.5.0`), which is the advisory's fixed release, confirming it is not the same vulnerable code path as the pre-0.31.5.0 backup restore flaw.
